resolution: Exact fractional-tail weighted CVaR implemented for NumPy, Torch, and device-resident paths; unequal-weight edge-case tests added.
Versioned IEEE case-specific shunt controls preserve fixed reactors/capacitors and export absolute/delta semantics.
Every supported case exports checksum, dimension, controls, bounds, steps, initial values, and fixed-device treatment in a formulation manifest.
Persistent XPU progress callback now reads iteration from payload; bounded dictionary-frame protocol and multi-frame tests added.
Publication objective statistics require independently verified, feasible, finite runs; feasibility and violation summaries are separate.
Article package aborts on incomplete validation and all publication narrative/tables/figures use verified-only data.
Campaigns preserve exactly requested 30/31/35/50 repetitions through CUSTOM portfolio synchronization; regression tests added.
Independent PYPOWER validation starts from the original controlled case with its own Q-limit enforcement and compares final bus types, Q, voltage, angle, and losses.
Scenario mode/count/parameters/topology are centrally validated; empty sets are rejected and scenario seeds, weights, transformed-case checksums, and manifest hash are persisted.
Mandatory-dependency headless suite passes 176 tests; GUI workflow tests are synchronized; CI covers Python 3.11-3.13 on Linux and Windows.
Case validation is mandatory for repository/CLI construction and active zero-impedance branches are rejected instead of electrically deleted.

All 20 optimizers have frozen seeded short-run snapshots, exact budget checks, normalized-bound checks, and deterministic release regression tests. Independent literature-equation re-derivation remains a continuing scientific review activity.

Scientific regressions cover all four case profiles/base flows, mixed-vector CPU/Torch parity on case30/57, fixed case118 reactors, robust scenarios, exact CVaR, zero impedance, and independent PYPOWER checks. Physical CUDA/XPU parity remains target-hardware validation.
Application-only line coverage increased from 37% to 62%; CI enforces fail-under=60. High-risk publication exporter is 98% covered; next target remains 75%+ and deeper publication-evidence branches.
Bootstrap reads installed distribution version with a 3.4.0 fallback, so version-transition prerequisite checks are not stale.
Release/freeze identity centralized at 3.4.0/calo_v34_freeze and campaign/GUI/CLI output paths updated.
Stale generated publication artifacts removed; source tree contains only a runtime-generation README and no pre-populated claims.
Developer absolute paths sanitized; zero-data historical snapshots explicitly marked disabled/not applied.

Model-only checkpoints use weights_only=True and optional hashes; resumable full-state loads require SHA-256 sidecars; local worker frames are bounded/schema-checked and documented trusted-same-host. Pickle remains only inside the local child-process trust boundary.
Exact CPU audit pins and Linux/Windows Python 3.11-3.13 CI added with compile, Ruff, tests, 60% coverage gate, and build. Hardware-specific Torch wheels remain bootstrap-selected by detected runtime.
Runtime blockers were isolated and tested, but large orchestration modules remain candidates for a later planner/scheduler/executor decomposition; this does not block v3.4 numerical correctness.
Repository-wide Ruff check and compileall pass and are mandatory CI gates.
Font availability is preflighted, explicit fallback is used without bundling proprietary files, and resolved font metadata is saved.

The supplied archive had no Git history, which cannot be reconstructed. v3.4 records the source-archive SHA-256, freeze hash, repository file manifest, build hashes, and explicitly marks commit/tag unavailable.
